title: Austin Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Hits 10 Model Y Vehicles
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet reached 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, the highest confirmed count for driverless service in the city.
impact_reason: Mildly positive growth in Austin's unsupervised fleet size, incrementally boosting local scaling capacity and real-world testing.

title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Four Unsupervised Vehicles Each
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data indicates Tesla deployed four unsupervised vehicles in Dallas and four in Houston shortly after launch, compared to 13 active unsupervised in Austin recently.
impact_reason: Mildly positive confirmation of initial fleet buildup in new cities, supporting gradual scaling velocity tied to Austin operations.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla announced the rollout of unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston, Texas, with initial geofence areas defined and video evidence showing no safety drivers. This triples Tesla's active Texas hubs alongside Austin.
impact_reason: Major positive catalyst as it directly expands unsupervised operations to two new major Texas cities, significantly accelerating overall robotaxi scaling momentum in the state including Austin.

title: Tesla Austin Robotaxis Involved in 14 Crashes Since Launch
summary: A February filing disclosed that Tesla's Austin robotaxis have been in 14 crashes since launch, amid expansion to new cities.
impact_reason: Mildly negative due to safety incidents that could invite regulatory scrutiny or public concern, potentially slowing unsupervised fleet scaling in Austin.
